purpose: Iterative random recursive subdivision of a flat plane. Each iteration randomly extrudes a subset of polygons inward (creating nested smaller polys) AND outward (raising blocks). Result: fractal-like nested-blocks landscape where every level of recursion has its own depth.
visual_result: Square plane evolves into nested-blocks fractal. At Iterations=1 it's a single coarse split; at Iterations=10 it's deeply nested. Heights animate over time via the Speed parameter (random seed offsets per frame).
input_geometry: Plane (5168) primitive — base mesh primitive feeding the Nodes Mesh capsule.

hosts.role: twin spline branch — captures recursion edges as splines for sweep render

annotations.text: There is an initial update issue. To solve this: Go inside the 'Loop Carried Value' Node and rewire the first wire between the 'Variable 1' Input and the 'Explode Mesh Island' Node. Have fun :)

annotation_storage_pattern: Annotation tag (type 1030659) on top-level INFO Null; read via tag[c4d.DescID(c4d.DescLevel(10014, 130, 1030659))].

am_sliders.name: Global Seed
am_sliders.controls: macro arrangement of where recursion lands

am_sliders.name: Scale
am_sliders.controls: noise sampler frequency driving extrude amounts
am_sliders.observed: low=large smooth blocks, high=small chaotic blocks

am_sliders.name: Speed
am_sliders.controls: animation rate over time — multiplies the seed offset per frame

key_connections.purpose: feed initial geometry into the iterator
drive number of LCV iterations from the artist slider
binds the iteration scope to the LCV body
iteration-aware random number per LCV step
fan out the iterated mesh by connected components
rebuild geometry from accumulated per-island arrays

output_routing.host_visibility_path: Nodes Mesh capsule (180420600) renders root output as classic mesh in OM
output_routing.notes: set_property is the canonical final tag-and-emit pattern in the net.maxon.nodespace.scene engine.

interesting_types: Geometry stream with multiple connected-component islands (after explode_islands)
Array-of-iteration accumulator (4× readvalueatindex + buildfromvalue) — collects per-iteration state for later inspection
LCV's auxiliary in@hash... ports — passthroughs for Hash/Seed/Speed/Scale/Global-Seed available inside LCV body each iteration

core_idea_nodes.name: group@<hash>
core_idea_nodes.why_it_matters: THE node that does the work. Iterates geometry through Iterations steps. Inside its body, an extrude with inset=100 creates a new poly INSIDE each existing poly each iteration — pure extrude-with-full-inset implements recursive nesting WITHOUT any subdivide-the-mesh op.

core_idea_nodes.name: explode_islands
core_idea_nodes.why_it_matters: After the LCV produces the recursive geometry, fan-out by connected component lets each recursion island be post-processed independently. The canonical 'do something per island' pattern.

core_idea_nodes.name: set_property
core_idea_nodes.why_it_matters: The canonical final-tag-and-emit node before root.geometryout. Tags the output with named selections/properties for downstream Material restriction.

what_is_clever: The LCV body uses extrude with inset=100 — each iteration creates a new poly INSIDE each existing poly. After N iterations you have nested polys at N levels of depth. No actual subdivide-the-mesh op is needed; pure extrude with full inset does it.
explode_islands after LCV splits the result into independent pieces so each can be post-processed independently. A 'fan-out' pattern that lets each recursion island be its own object.
Twin graphs: the Nodes Spline (180420700) sibling captures the same recursive geometry as splines for sweeping in the render variant. Single visual concept, two stream types (mesh + spline).
Array-of-iteration accumulators (buildfromvalue + 4× readvalueatindex) collect per-iteration state into arrays. Post-LCV chain reads what the LCV did via array introspection rather than re-iterating.
Auxiliary LCV passthroughs (in@hash...) — Hash/Seed/Speed/Scale/Global-Seed available inside LCV body each iteration without re-wiring.

gotchas.issue: On fresh load, LCV's current._0 doesn't bind until manually re-wired
gotchas.evidence: Author left an Annotation tag explicitly documenting this: 'go inside the LCV node and rewire the first wire between Variable 1 Input and Explode Mesh Island Node'
gotchas.workaround: Open the LCV editor, rewire the first wire to re-trigger the binding. Author-documented in the scene itself.

rebuild_recipe: 1. Create a Nodes Mesh (180420600). Add a Plane primitive node inside; wire plane.geometryout → next stage.
2. Create a Loop Carried Value node with types=[Geometry, Geometry]. Source ← plane.geometryout.
3. Inside the LCV body, place an extrude node with inset=100; wire current._0 → extrude.in, extrude.out → next._0.
4. Drive the LCV's iteration count with a Range node whose end ← root.Iterations (synthesized AM port).
5. Wire range.innerdomain → group(LCV).innerdomain so the LCV scope tracks the range.
6. Add hash node: hash.seed ← range.index (per-iteration random), hash.salt ← root.Global Seed.
7. Use hash output to drive extrude.depth (so each iteration's depth varies).
8. After the LCV: fan out via explode_islands → containeriteration.
9. Accumulate per-island data with buildfromvalue + 4× readvalueatindex.
10. Reassemble geometry via assembler → splinechamfer → extrudeline → tessellation chain.
12. Synthesize 6 typed AM ports on root: Iterations(Int32), Global Seed(Int32), Noise Type(enum), Scale(Float), Seed(Float), Speed(Float).

minimal_reproducible_subgraph.name: extrude_inset_lcv_recursion
minimal_reproducible_subgraph.purpose: Recursively subdivide a polygonal surface by repeated full-inset extrude inside an LCV. Applies to ANY input mesh.
nodes: range (drives iteration count from AM Iterations slider)
minimal_reproducible_subgraph.value_proposition: Lets ANY mesh be fed into a recursive nested-poly generator. Tiny graph (7 nodes); visually dramatic across the Iterations range. Generalizes to: hierarchical city-block generation, fractal terrain, recursive panel breakdowns, voxel-style nested-grid layouts.

lessons_for_cinema4d_mcp: set_property is the canonical 'final tag + emit' node — used on the geometry just before root.geometryout to tag the result with any final named selection or property. Should be in any output chain.
explode_islands + containeriteration is the canonical 'do something per island/component' pattern. Worth recipe R5b.

Author-documented bugs in scene annotations are real signal — Maxon's framework has a load-order edge case where LCV's current._0/next._0 self-binding doesn't activate until manually re-engaged. Useful gotcha for procedural-tool shipping (an asset saved cold may need a re-wire pass on load).
Twin-graph pattern (Nodes Mesh + Nodes Spline of equivalent shape) is a recurring DRuckli pattern — render variants often need both mesh and edge-spline outputs.
